title: Lemma C: Cauchy-Schwarz / variational step yielding RECIPROCAL additivity

comments.author: human
comments.body:
From the score-projection identity (Lemma B) one must derive the **reciprocal-additive** form $1/\Phi_n(p\boxplus q)\ge 1/\Phi_n(p)+1/\Phi_n(q)$, not a mere subadditivity.

**Required:** the standard Blachman/Voiculescu optimization. With $v=\mathrm{score}(p)$, $w=\mathrm{score}(q)$ and $u=\mathrm{score}(p\boxplus q)$ expressed via the contraction $\Pi$, minimize $\|s\,(\text{lift of }v)+t\,(\text{lift of }w)\|^2$ over scalars with $s+t$ (or appropriate) fixed, and apply Cauchy-Schwarz to obtain $\|u\|^{-2}\ge\|v\|^{-2}+\|w\|^{-2}$.

**Difficulty:** reciprocal additivity (as opposed to subadditivity) requires $\Pi$ to be an *orthogonal projection* (idempotent + self-adjoint) under the chosen metric. The exact inner product and optimal weights $s,t$ must be pinned down; this is fully determined by the precise form Lemma B takes.

**Edge case:** the optimization is undefined when $\Phi_n(p)=0$ (zero score vector, e.g. $n=1$). Handle separately.
